pi_items.source: Web search verification: DSM-5 code 314.01 applies when both inattention and hyperactivity-impulsivity criteria are met for at least 6 months. ICD-10-CM code F90.2 is the equivalent coding for combined type ADHD.

privileged_info_summary: This task involves a synthetic psychological evaluation case for a 12-year-old patient named Marcus Thorne (DOB: 2012-05-14). The patient is diagnosed with ADHD Combined Presentation (DSM-5: 314.01, ICD-10: F90.2) with moderate severity. The WISC-V index scores (Verbal Comprehension: 112, Visual Spatial: 98, Fluid Reasoning: 105, Working Memory: 88, Processing Speed: 85) with corresponding percentiles are mathematically accurate. The task requires creating a clinical report document with specific formatting (centered header 'CONFIDENTIAL - PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION' on every page), a table of the five WISC-V index scores, patient identifying information, reason for referral (attention difficulties and academic underperformance in mathematics), and diagnostic impression including the DSM-5 code 314.01.
